steps.title: Identify the research promise
steps.content: The passage sets up a problem: current herbicides kill harmless plants nearby. The solution being researched is engineered DNA that would offer exclusively targeting E. esula—a way to affect only the invasive plant, not others.

steps.title: Match the consequence
steps.content:
The logical consequence of exclusively targeting E. esula is reducing its numbers without harming other organisms—solving the collateral damage problem that current herbicides create.

The other three either ignore this exclusive-targeting advantage, contradict the passage's claims, or focus on a different issue.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
